2605.14786.title: Known By Their Actions: Fingerprinting LLM Browser Agents via UI Traces
2605.14786.paper_summary: As LLM-based agents increasingly browse the web on users' behalf, a natural question arises: can websites passively identify which underlying model powers an agent? Doing so would represent a significant security risk, enabling targeted attacks tailored to known model vulnerabilities. Across 14 frontier LLMs and four web environments spanning information retrieval and shopping tasks, we show that an agent's actions and interaction timings, captured via a passive JavaScript tracker, are sufficient to identify the underlying model with up to 96\% F1. We formalise this attack surface by demonstrating that classifiers trained on agent actions generalise across model sizes and families. We further show that strong classifiers can be trained from few interaction traces and that agent identity can be inferred early within an episode. Injecting randomised timing delays between actions substantially degrades classifier performance, but does not provide robust protection: a classifier retrained on delayed traces largely recovers performance. We release our harness and a labelled corpus of agent traces \href{https://github.com/KabakaWilliam/known_actions}{here}.
2605.14786.paper_summary_zh: 隨著基於LLM的代理越來越多地代表用戶瀏覽網頁，一個自然的問題出現了：網站能否被動識別出驅動代理的底層模型？這樣做將代表一個重大的安全風險，使得針對已知模型漏洞的定向攻擊成為可能。在14個前沿LLM和四個涵蓋信息檢索及購物任務的網頁環境中，我們展示了通過被動的JavaScript追蹤器捕捉到的代理行為和互動時間足以以高達96\%的F1識別底層模型。我們通過展示在代理行為上訓練的分類器如何在模型大小和家族之間進行泛化來正式化這一攻擊面。我們進一步顯示，強大的分類器可以從少量的互動痕跡中訓練出來，並且代理身份可以在一個回合內早期推斷出來。在行為之間注入隨機的時間延遲會顯著降低分類器的性能，但並未提供穩健的保護：在延遲痕跡上重新訓練的分類器性能大部分恢復。我們在這裡釋放我們的工具和標記的代理痕跡語料庫 \href{https://github.com/KabakaWilliam/known_actions}{here}。
2605.14786.author: William Lugoloobi et.al.
2605.14786.authors: William Lugoloobi, Samuelle Marro, Jabez Magomere, Joss Wright, Chris Russell
